reasoning:
The threshold value for this forecast (the last published value as of April 13, 2025) is the April 9 reading of 534,253 million. This value is slightly elevated compared to March baselines (~522k) due to lingering effects of the Q1 quarter-end spike. 
1. April 20 (Reflects April 16 data): Tax day (April 15) typically causes a draw on MMFs and RRP as liquidity is pulled for tax settlements, and the quarter-end effect will have further dissipated. Thus, a continued short-term decline is highly likely (p=0.28).
2. May 13 (Reflects May 7 data): While there is a historical tendency for a seasonal bump in May as the Treasury shifts its bill issuance post-tax season, the ongoing backdrop of Quantitative Tightening (QT) exerts negative pressure. (p=0.37).
3. July 12 & Oct 10: As we look further ahead, uncertainty surrounding the exact timing of the Fed ending QT increases significantly. The downward structural trend will weaken as ON RRP approaches zero, leaving mainly the stable/fluctuating Foreign Repo pool (~$350B-$400B). Following system instructions to strictly increase uncertainty (regress toward 0.5) for distant horizons, probabilities are set to 0.43 and 0.48 respectively.
